message:
fix(raptorq): detect inconsistent overdetermined systems in Gaussian elimination

Previously, when an overdetermined equation system had contradictory
constraints (e.g., x=0x10, y=0x20, x+y=0x31 where 0x10^0x20=0x30≠0x31),
the decoder would silently accept a corrupt solution because the all-zero
coefficient rows with nonzero RHS were never checked after elimination.

This commit adds explicit inconsistency detection at three levels:

1. GaussianSolver (`linalg.rs`):
   - New `GaussianResult::Inconsistent { row }` variant distinguishes
     "no pivot found" (Singular) from "0 = b, b≠0" (Inconsistent)
   - `first_inconsistent_row()` scans post-elimination rows for the
     contradictory witness
   - Both `solve()` and `solve_markowitz()` check for inconsistency
     after the forward-elimination phase completes
   - All existing match arms updated to handle the new variant

2. InactivationDecoder (`decoder.rs`):
   - `first_inconsistent_dense_row()` performs the same check on the
     dense sub-matrix used by the inactivation path
   - `inconsistent_matrix_error()` maps the dense row index back to
     the original equation ID via `unused_eqs`
   - Both `inactivate_and_solve` and `inactivate_and_solve_with_proof`
     check immediately before extracting solutions
   - `DecodeError::SingularMatrix::row` doc updated to clarify it can
     represent either a missing-pivot column or an inconsistency witness

3. Differential harness (`raptorq_conformance.rs`):
   - `GaussianResult::Inconsistent` mapped to `ReferenceDecodeError::SingularMatrix`
     to keep the reference decoder's error surface unchanged
   - `PeriodicSummaryInput` changed from Copy to Clone (was blocking
     compilation due to unrelated field addition)

New tests:
- `gaussian_inconsistent_overdetermined_matrix_detected`: verifies both
  basic and Markowitz solvers report the contradictory row
- `inconsistent_overdetermined_system_reports_singular_error`: verifies
  the inactivation decoder returns a deterministic witness row
- `inconsistent_overdetermined_with_proof_preserves_attempt_history`:
  verifies the proof path preserves the full pivot-event sequence

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
